macro_segments.title: Introduction to Meilah: Basic Concepts and Categories

micro_segments.title: Opening remarks on Daf Gimmel and textual characteristics of Meilah
Definition and parameters of Meila d'Oraisa: korban, asham meilos, and chomesh
Distinction between meila and other issurim; narrow definition of lo ma'al
Meila d'rabbanan: expanded application without korban or chomesh
Conceptual frameworks for meila: stealing vs. deriving unauthorized benefit
Shiur of meila: prutha as monetary measure and benefit-based assessment
Kedoshei Hashem as prerequisite for meila; objects and kedusha rather than act of meila
Classifications of korbanot by meila status: olas vs. shelamim vs. chatas and asham

macro_segments.title: Psulo Bakodesh and the Principle of עלו לא ירדו: Testing Kedoshei Hashem Status

micro_segments.title: Overview of yesterday's debate between Rava and Rav Yosef on whether psulo bakodesh korbanot descend from mizbeiach
Chada migu chada: stringing three mishnayos to establish psulo bakodesh principle
First mishna: shechting bird incorrectly; lo mitame begadim despite psul melika
Second mishna: psulah bakodesh defined as technical ritual error, not fundamental disqualification
Third mishna: if psulah bakodesh, alu lo yardu—stays on mizbeiach once placed
Rav Yosef's challenge to Rava using the three mishnayos: logical proof that alu lo yardu implies psulo bakodesh status
Resolution: Rava concedes; alu lo yardu test confirms kedoshei Hashem status for meila

macro_segments.title: Rabbi Elazar's Question on Olat Bamos Yachid: Sanctity and Status Transfer to Merkaz

micro_segments.title: Introduction to the case of olah sanctified for private bama brought to central mizbeiach
Historical context: she'at heter bamos when mishkan existed but central beis hamikdash did not
Kaltuha mechitzos principle: walls of central mizbeiach absorb korban brought from bama jurisdiction
Rabbi Elazar's question: if olah sanctified for bama is disqualified upon entering merkaz, does it descend?
Interpretation: the question presumes standard case (shechita b'darom) is resolved; special bama case is contested
Two interpretive paths: leniency via original bama status, or stringency via loss of merkaz identity
Gemara response teiku: no resolution; debate remains open whether bama-sanctified korban retains alu lo yardu

macro_segments.title: Rav Gidel's Statement on Zrikas Piggul and Meila Status of Kodashim

micro_segments.title: Rav Gidel amar Rav: zrikas piggul does not remove kodashim from meila category
Application to kodashei kodashim: meat remains meila despite psul machshava at zrika
Application to kodashim kalim: imurin do not become meila without valid zrika
Tosafos: chiddush addresses machshava of piggul at zrika moment; principle extends prior understanding

macro_segments.title: Rav Pappa's Challenge via Mishna of Toda and Shechita B'Piggul

micro_segments.title: Mishna case: toda with bread sanctified at shechita; shechita b'piggul still sanctifies bread (kidesh ha-lechem)
Inference from mishna: shechitas piggul does affect kedoshim status, contradicting Rav Gidel's statement
Bread never becomes meila since it is muttar b'achila; analogy argument only about kedusha impact
Abaye's silence and consultation with Rabbi Abba

macro_segments.title: The Role of Zrika vs. Shechita in Piggul Status: Timing and Retroactive Labeling

micro_segments.title: Rav Ashi's response: difference lies at moment of zrika, not shechita; piggul status hinges on zrika completion
Rav Ashi's challenge to the explanation: kometz piggul is labeled piggul before zrika, contradicting the prior answer
Gemara's response: we call it kometz piggul meaning it will become piggul, not that it is piggul yet
Ravina's objection: use case from Zevachim of debate on two separate acts (shechita and zrika with conflicting machshavos)
Ilfa's distinction: piggul status contested when two avodos have different machshavos; unanimous in one avoda
Gemara: order and timing of machshavos matter; piggul label comes retroactively at shechita moment after zrika completes
Concession: piggul eventually applies retroactively; return to bread case: how does shechitas piggul sanctify bread?
Final answer: shechitas piggul sanctifies only for kedusha purposes (psulo balina), not for meila; meila requires complete valid avodah
